SEARCH
Package server.net

Class OrderServer

java.lang.Object
server.net.OrderServer
public class OrderServer extends Object
The TCP server: accepts client connections on a port, spawns a handler thread per client that reads ClientRequests in a loop and dispatches them through a RequestRouter, and runs the SchedulerService timed jobs for its lifetime. Lifecycle events and per-request activity are reported through a ServerListener. Handler failures are contained per request so one bad request never drops the whole connection.

  • Field Summary

    Fields
    Modifier and Type
    Field
    Description
    private final List<Socket>
    activeClients
    Every accepted client socket, so stop() can force-close them.
    private final ServerListener
    listener
    Receives lifecycle, connection, and activity events.
    private final int
    port
    The TCP port to listen on.
    private final RequestRouter
    router
    Shared request dispatcher, one instance across all client threads.
    private boolean
    running
    Whether the accept loop is running.
    private final SchedulerService
    scheduler
    The timed-job runner, started and stopped with the server.
    private ServerSocket
    serverSocket
    The listening server socket, or null while stopped.

  • Constructor Summary

    Constructors
    Constructor
    Description
    OrderServer(int port, ServerListener listener)
    Creates a server bound to a port and reporting to a listener.

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    SchedulerService
    getScheduler()
    The server's timed-job runner — exposed so the server console can wire its manual "run now" triggers to SchedulerService.runNow(String) / SchedulerService.runAllNow().
    private void
    handleClient(Socket socket, String ip, String host)
    Per-client handler thread: reads requests in a loop until disconnect, dispatching each through the router and writing the response, with per-request failure containment and session/lock cleanup on teardown.
    private void
    runAcceptLoop()
    Accept loop: binds the socket and spawns a handler thread per client.
    void
    start()
    Starts the accept loop (on a daemon thread) and the timed-job scheduler.
    void
    stop()
    Stops the scheduler, closes the listening socket, and force-closes every still-open client connection so their handler threads unwind.

    Methods inherited from class java.lang.Object

    clone, equals, finalize, getClass, hashCode, notify, notifyAll, toString, wait, wait, wait

      The listening server socket, or null while stopped.

      Whether the accept loop is running.

      The timed-job runner, started and stopped with the server.

  • Constructor Details

    • OrderServer

      public OrderServer(int port, ServerListener listener)
      Creates a server bound to a port and reporting to a listener.
      Parameters:
      port - the TCP port to listen on
      listener - receives lifecycle, connection, and activity events

  • Method Details

    • start

      public void start()
      Starts the accept loop (on a daemon thread) and the timed-job scheduler.

    • getScheduler

      public SchedulerService getScheduler()
      The server's timed-job runner — exposed so the server console can wire its manual "run now" triggers to SchedulerService.runNow(String) / SchedulerService.runAllNow().
      Returns:
      this server's scheduler

    • stop

      public void stop()
      Stops the scheduler, closes the listening socket, and force-closes every still-open client connection so their handler threads unwind.

    • runAcceptLoop

      private void runAcceptLoop()
      Accept loop: binds the socket and spawns a handler thread per client.

    • handleClient

      private void handleClient(Socket socket, String ip, String host)
      Per-client handler thread: reads requests in a loop until disconnect, dispatching each through the router and writing the response, with per-request failure containment and session/lock cleanup on teardown.
      Parameters:
      socket - the client socket
      ip - the client's IP address
      host - the client's resolved host name
